areas.name: Bridge
areas.exteriorPlacement: Forward/dorsal command placement on the broad main hull
hardFacts: The bridge tracks convoy position, quarantine state, crop stores, medical load, and threat arcs together.
constraints: Bridge scenes should turn care and deterrence into accountable orders.

areas.name: Ready room and briefing room
hardFacts: The briefing room can become a contested aid office where true priorities cannot all win.
constraints: Humanitarian planning should remain physically tied to ship stores, quarantine, and escort limits.

areas.name: Mission ops and public-accounting room
hardFacts: Mission ops makes relief math visible and challengeable.
constraints: Visible fairness can create panic when there is not enough.

areas.name: Sickbay and quarantine ward
hardFacts: Sickbay and quarantine are shipboard borders: help may require sealed doors, refused contact, or transport denial.
constraints: Quarantine should have physical flow, logs, pass-throughs, and emotional cost.

areas.name: Biological and agricultural labs
hardFacts: Lab delay can be care: certification, seed testing, and contamination review decide whether relief is safe.
constraints: Science can look like delay when cargo holds are full of urgent need.

areas.name: Relief cargo, seed vaults, and field stores
hardFacts: Clean seed, medicine, and cargo are finite and carry temperature, viability, destination, custody, and public-accounting constraints.
constraints: Replicators cannot erase agricultural scale, seed viability, local autonomy, or public trust problems.

areas.name: Aft centerline shuttlebay and intake screening
areas.exteriorPlacement: Aft centerline bay opening into the gap between wing-shaped warp pylons; approach from astern through the pylon gap
hardFacts: Norway small-craft recovery uses the aft pylon-gap shuttlebay in Directive baseline.
Small craft approach from astern through the gap between wing-shaped warp pylons.
The bay links directly to intake screening, decon, cargo, and medical routes.
constraints: Do not depict a saucer-underbelly, forward bow, or Galaxy-style dorsal shuttlebay.
Pylon field stress, shields, decon readiness, and cargo/medical traffic can affect recovery timing.

areas.name: Main engineering and pylon field support
areas.zone: Decks 10-11 aft/lower engineering spaces near pylon roots
areas.exteriorPlacement: Aft/lower support spaces tied to wing-pylon field geometry and keel access
hardFacts: Humanitarian systems compete for power: shields, quarantine fields, cargo refrigeration, transporters, grow cabinets, decon pumps, and warp speed.
constraints: Engineering should turn relief promises into power distribution and accepted risk.

areas.name: Transporter, security, and controlled-access spaces
hardFacts: Security protects aid, quarantine, patients, and public order; it is not punitive by default.
constraints: The ship must be open enough to help and closed enough to prevent collapse.

areas.name: Lower support and keel maintenance
areas.zone: Lower keel, environmental, sanitation, waste, and pylon/structural access
areas.exteriorPlacement: Lower keel and pylon-root support structures
hardFacts: Relief can fail because of filters, pumps, waste processors, decon lines, or environmental systems.
constraints: Unglamorous support work should matter as much as public speeches when relief systems strain.

systems.name: Bow phaser emitter and defensive posture
systems.scope: Dorsal bow tactical emitter and defensive command posture
systems.capability: Visible deterrence that protects relief operations and complicates how help is perceived.

systems.name: Quarantine and decontamination systems
systems.scope: Medical, lab, shuttlebay, and controlled-access decon flow
systems.capability: Uses fields, doors, pass-throughs, logs, and sanitation systems to prevent exposure from becoming spread.

systems.name: Relief cargo, seed custody, and cold chain
systems.scope: Central mission-spine cargo and biological support spaces
systems.capability: Transports, protects, verifies, and distributes finite relief supplies and biological materials.

systems.name: Pylon field support and compact power grid
systems.scope: Wing-pylon warp field, aft bay geometry, and shipwide power distribution
systems.capability: Coordinates warp field stability, shields, shuttlebay approach, and relief system power under compact-grid limits.

systems.name: Public accounting and relief legitimacy
systems.scope: Mission ops ledgers, supply boards, public-facing records, and command authorization
systems.capability: Makes scarce relief choices visible, inspectable, and challengeable enough to sustain trust.

refs: 2. Class Personality
9. Portrayal Principles

payload.summary: U.S.S. Celandine is a Norway-class vessel. A Norway-class ship is a compact 2370s Starfleet response vessel whose visible combat authority protects relief, biological science, convoy work, and public-accounting missions without replacing local institutions. Campaign use: U.S.S. Celandine: Cyradon relief, agricultural science, seed custody, quarantine, crop transition, and convoy protection.
constraints: Do not treat the class as a hospital ship with no tactical edge or as a Defiant-style gunboat.
Replicators do not erase agricultural, seed, medicine, or regional logistics scarcity.
hardAnchors: Broad flat-iron hull with Defiant-like keel influence.
Wing-shaped warp pylons create an aft centerline gap.
Directive baseline places the shuttlebay at the aft centerline, approached from astern through the pylon gap.
Dorsal bow phaser emitter is a class tell.

refs: 3.1 Silhouette and first read
3.2 Exterior zones

payload.summary: The Norway exterior reads broad, flat, and purposeful: flat-iron hull, Defiant-like keel, wing-shaped pylons with aft gap, dorsal bow phaser emitter, and service/cargo locks for relief work.
constraints: Exterior prose should keep the pylon gap, bow emitter, and flat hull visible when relevant.
hardAnchors: Wing-shaped pylons and aft gap shape small-craft approach.
The dorsal bow phaser emitter should appear as deterrence and perception pressure.
Relief posture should be visible through cargo locks, sanitation fields, and convoy shielding.

refs: 3.3 Shuttle approach truth
5.7 Aft centerline shuttlebay and intake screening, Deck 9

payload.summary: The aft centerline shuttlebay is both welcome door and contamination boundary, reached from astern through the pylon gap. Norway small-craft recovery uses the aft pylon-gap shuttlebay in Directive baseline. Small craft approach from astern through the gap between wing-shaped warp pylons. The bay links directly to intake screening, decon, cargo, and medical routes.
constraints: Do not depict a saucer-underbelly, forward bow, or Galaxy-style dorsal shuttlebay.
Pylon field stress, shields, decon readiness, and cargo/medical traffic can affect recovery timing.
hardAnchors: Norway small-craft recovery uses the aft pylon-gap shuttlebay in Directive baseline.
Small craft approach from astern through the gap between wing-shaped warp pylons.
The bay links directly to intake screening, decon, cargo, and medical routes.
avoid: Do not depict a saucer-underbelly, forward bow, or Galaxy-style dorsal shuttlebay.

hardAnchors: The bridge tracks convoy position, quarantine state, crop stores, medical load, and threat arcs together.

cards.title: Bow phaser emitter and defensive posture

refs: 6.1 Bow phaser emitter and defensive posture

payload.summary: The bow emitter protects relief but can make help feel coercive.
constraints: Capability: Visible deterrence that protects relief operations and complicates how help is perceived.
Dependencies: rules of engagement; shield state; convoy proximity; public trust; power reserve.
Failure modes: escalation; trust loss; heat stress; defensive blind spot.
hardAnchors: Dorsal bow tactical emitter and defensive command posture
Visible deterrence that protects relief operations and complicates how help is perceived.

refs: 6.2 Quarantine and decontamination systems

payload.summary: Quarantine is a physical, ethical, and logistical system rather than a generic locked door.
constraints: Capability: Uses fields, doors, pass-throughs, logs, and sanitation systems to prevent exposure from becoming spread.
Dependencies: medical authority; decon pumps; traffic flow; sensor screening; security checkpoints.
Failure modes: breach; false negative; public panic; isolation strain; pump failure.
hardAnchors: Medical, lab, shuttlebay, and controlled-access decon flow
Uses fields, doors, pass-throughs, logs, and sanitation systems to prevent exposure from becoming spread.

cards.title: Relief cargo, seed custody, and cold chain

refs: 6.3 Relief cargo, seed custody, and cold chain

payload.summary: Cargo is a regional life-support system with temperature, custody, destination, viability, and legitimacy.
constraints: Capability: Transports, protects, verifies, and distributes finite relief supplies and biological materials.
Dependencies: environmental control; public accounting; decon flow; cargo security; lab verification.
Failure modes: viability loss; theft; contamination; public legitimacy collapse; allocation conflict.
hardAnchors: Central mission-spine cargo and biological support spaces
Transports, protects, verifies, and distributes finite relief supplies and biological materials.

cards.title: Pylon field support and compact power grid

refs: 6.4 Pylon field support and compact power grid

payload.summary: The pylon gap and power grid are operational constraints, not decoration.
constraints: Capability: Coordinates warp field stability, shields, shuttlebay approach, and relief system power under compact-grid limits.
Dependencies: pylon integrity; warp field control; shuttle approach window; cargo environmental load; shield demand.
Failure modes: pylon stress; bay approach delay; power brownout; shield compromise; warp limit.
hardAnchors: Wing-pylon warp field, aft bay geometry, and shipwide power distribution
Coordinates warp field stability, shields, shuttlebay approach, and relief system power under compact-grid limits.

refs: 6.5 Public accounting

payload.summary: Public accounting lets relief choices become playable pressure instead of hidden math.
constraints: Capability: Makes scarce relief choices visible, inspectable, and challengeable enough to sustain trust.
Dependencies: accurate inventory; local data; command authorization; communications; crew transparency discipline.
Failure modes: rumor; favoritism allegation; panic; hidden stock accusation; legitimacy collapse.
hardAnchors: Mission ops ledgers, supply boards, public-facing records, and command authorization
Makes scarce relief choices visible, inspectable, and challengeable enough to sustain trust.
